items.myth: The most capable Claude tier is always the best choice.
items.reality: Higher tiers trade cost and latency for depth—many tasks run well on Sonnet or Haiku with clearer prompts.
items.example: Rewriting a short email on Opus when Haiku would suffice doubles cost without meaningful quality gain.

items.myth: Claude's coding strength means generated code is production-ready without review.
items.reality: Claude accelerates drafting and explanation; humans or CI must still run tests, security review, and linting.
items.example: A suggested SQL migration may omit an index—running `EXPLAIN` and tests catches the gap before deploy.
